project.name: metapak
project.description: Universal terminal-based (TUI) package manager in Rust — unifies search, install, and removal across system and ecosystem package managers through a single keyboard-driven interface.
project.author: sreevarshan-xenoz

src/lib.rs.description: Declares all public modules and re-exports the main library entry point

src/main.rs.description: CLI argument parsing (clap), initializes config, sets up terminal, runs the App event loop via App::run()

src/app.rs.description: Central application state: holds all UI state, search state, selected packages, pagination, filtering, sorting, mode switching, and the main run() event loop

src/ui.rs.description: All TUI rendering logic: layouts, widgets, status bars, help overlays, popups, system info views, dependency trees, health dashboards

src/services.rs.description: Unified package service with built-in providers (Pacman, AUR, Npm, Cargo, Pip). Delegates to providers, aggregates results, coordinates search/install/remove

src/backends/mod.rs.description: Minimal module that only re-exports the snapshots submodule

src/backends/snapshots/mod.rs.description: Declares snapshot provider modules (btrfs, timeshift) and the SnapshotProvider trait

src/backends/snapshots/btrfs.rs.description: Btrfs snapshot management via subprocess (btrfs, snapper). Create, list, rollback snapshots

src/backends/snapshots/timeshift.rs.description: Timeshift snapshot management via timeshift CLI. Create, list, rollback snapshots

src/traits.rs.description: Defines the PackageProvider trait (search, is_installed) and UpdateProvider trait (check_updates, get_outdated_packages). The heart of the provider abstraction layer

src/models.rs.description: Core data types: Package, OutdatedPackage, PackageSource (Pacman, Aur, Npm, Cargo, Pip)

src/config.rs.description: Toml-based config loading from ~/.config/metapak/config.toml. Migration from legacy 'arch-tui' config path. Config struct includes hooks, telemetry, color, debounce, snapshot, theme settings

src/constants.rs.description: Application-wide constants: app name, version indicators, config paths, debounce defaults, max results, terminal refresh rate, network config, search limits

src/action.rs.description: Defines Action enum — all possible user-triggered actions (Search, Install, Remove, Update, Quit, Refresh, Sort, Filter, Select, Navigate, etc.)

src/errors.rs.description: Centralized error types: AppError enum (Config, Io, Parse, Backend, Network, Permission, Snapshot, etc.) with conversion traits

src/input.rs.description: Keyboard input processing: normal mode keys, search mode text input, key event normalization (Crossterm events → internal Key enum)

src/keybindings.rs.description: Maps keys to Actions: configurable keybindings, default bindings (/, ?, q, Tab, Enter, Esc, h, j, k, d, v, f, s, u, i, o, p, c, G, g, I, F, O, P, C)

src/theme.rs.description: Two built-in themes: Catppuccin Mocha (dark) and Catppuccin Latte (light). Colors for all UI elements. Runtime switching via T key

src/ui_utils.rs.description: Helper functions for rendering: truncated text, styled blocks, progress bars, scrollable lists, formatted sizes, colored status badges

src/search.rs.description: Fuzzy matching and scoring with EnhancedSearch: parses queries with AND/OR/NOT operators and filters (repo:, aur:, installed:, etc.), ranks by relevance, regex support

src/parallel.rs.description: Async concurrent backend operations: runs search/install/remove across all active backends concurrently via tokio tasks. Cancellation support

src/operation_queue.rs.description: Batch operation orchestration: queues multiple package operations, resolves dependencies, orders operations, manages execution with progress tracking

src/simulation.rs.description: Pre-execution simulation: runs dry-run mode to show what operations would do before committing

src/transaction_manager.rs.description: Safe transactional execution: pre-snapshot, execute operations, verify success, rollback on failure, post-snapshot on success. Wraps operations in rollback-safe transactions

src/transaction_history.rs.description: Persistent log of all package operations: timestamps, package names, operation type, result status, duration. Structured log file with rotation

src/hooks.rs.description: Pre/post execution hooks: runs user-configured shell commands before and after package operations. Supports conditional hooks based on operation type

src/notifications.rs.description: Desktop notification dispatch: sends system notifications on operation completion (success/failure) via notify-rust or platform-native mechanisms

src/export.rs.description: Historically exported package lists. All contents removed during dead code cleanup — module retained only for structural compatibility

src/diagnostics.rs.description: System health checks: disk space, package manager status, mirror health, backend availability

src/dependency_visualization.rs.description: Interactive dependency tree visualization: builds dependency graph from package metadata, renders as collapsible tree in TUI

src/watchdog.rs.description: System health monitoring: disk usage checking, mirror latency tracking, GPG key verification. Provides HealthWatchdog struct with periodic checks

src/telemetry.rs.description: Structured operation logging: logs all package operations with metadata (timestamp, duration, backend, result)

src/animations.rs.description: TUI animation utilities: loading spinners, progress animations, transition effects for UI state changes

src/i18n.rs.description: Multi-language support: loads locale from environment, provides translated strings for UI labels, messages, errors. Auto-detection and fallback to English

src/utils.rs.description: General-purpose helper functions: string formatting, platform checks, path utilities, time formatting

src/platform.rs.description: OS/platform detection: identifies current OS (Linux/macOS/Windows), distribution (Arch/Debian/Fedora/etc.), and available package managers

src/security.rs.description: Basic security/verification utilities: validates command paths, checks permissions

layers.description: TUI rendering (ui.rs, ui_utils.rs) and keyboard input handling (input.rs, keybindings.rs, action.rs)
Central state machine (app.rs) — search state, package list, detail pane, mode switching
Package service (services.rs): coordinates all providers, parallel execution (parallel.rs), operation queue (operation_queue.rs)
Package search/filter/sort (search.rs), simulation (simulation.rs), transaction management (transaction_manager.rs), dependency visualization (dependency_visualization.rs)
5 built-in providers in services.rs (Pacman, AUR, npm, Cargo, pip) + 2 snapshot providers (btrfs, timeshift)

Command execution (command.rs), error handling (errors.rs), config (config.rs), telemetry (telemetry.rs), hooks (hooks.rs), notifications (notifications.rs), diagnostics (diagnostics.rs), watchdog (watchdog.rs), security (security.rs), platform detection (platform.rs)
Theme (theme.rs), i18n (i18n.rs), animations (animations.rs), constants (constants.rs), utils (utils.rs), transaction history (transaction_history.rs)

PackageProvider.description: Interface implemented by all 5 package providers

search_flow: User types query → input.rs parses key events → Action::Search(query) → app.rs updates SearchState
PackageService delegates to all 5 providers concurrently via tokio tasks
Each provider runs provider.search() → subprocess or reqwest HTTP call
Results returned as Vec<Package> → services.rs aggregates and deduplicates
search.rs EnhancedSearch applies query parsing + filtering + ranking
Results stored and rendered in TUI package list
install_flow: User selects packages → presses Enter → Action triggers transaction
TransactionManager executes with pre-snapshot (if configured), install/remove commands, post-snapshot
On failure: rollback via snapshot provider
Hooks: pre hooks → operations → post hooks

key_features.unified_search: Queries 5 package providers simultaneously, merges results, filters with query syntax (AND/OR/NOT/repo:/aur:), ranks by relevance
key_features.batch_operations: Select multiple packages with Tab, install/remove in one batched transaction
key_features.simulation_mode: Dry-run to preview what operations would do before committing
key_features.snapshot_rollback: Btrfs/Timeshift snapshot before operations, automatic rollback on failure
key_features.circuit_breaker: AUR API protection: disables AUR queries on error threshold, periodic recovery attempts
key_features.transaction_safety: Pre-check → snapshot → execute → verify → rollback-on-failure → post-snapshot pipeline
key_features.health_dashboard: Disk space, package manager status, mirror connectivity checks
key_features.dependency_visualization: Interactive dependency tree viewer with forward/reverse dependency traversal
key_features.theme_system: Catppuccin Mocha (dark) / Latte (light) runtime theme switching
key_features.i18n: Locale auto-detection with translated UI strings and fallback to English
key_features.pre_post_hooks: User-configurable shell commands executed before/after package operations
key_features.telemetry_logging: Structured operation logs with auto-rotation and configurable verbosity

integration_tests.description: Robustness, simulation, and snapshot integration tests

key_design_principles: Provider abstraction via PackageProvider trait — all 5 providers implement the same interface
Built-in providers in services.rs — no separate backend files, all providers defined inline
Fail-safe transactions — snapshot before, rollback on failure, post-snapshot on success
Parallel async execution — all providers queried concurrently via tokio tasks
Debounced search — avoids flooding providers with intermediate keystrokes
Circuit breaker pattern — protects AUR API from overload during outages
Separation of concerns — each source file has a focused responsibility
safety_patterns: Automatic retry logic for db locks and dependency errors
Snapshot-based rollback on transaction failure (transaction_manager.rs + snapshots)
Circuit breaker for external API protection (watchdog.rs)
Error type hierarchy for structured error handling (errors.rs)
